Clean_Code_SOLID.logic: Single Responsibility Principle: A class should have one reason to change. Decouple logic into specialized components.
Open-Closed Principle: Open for extension, closed for modification. Use interfaces and abstraction layers.
Liskov Substitution Principle: Objects of a superclass should be replaceable with objects of its subclasses without breaking the application.
Interface Segregation Principle: No client should be forced to depend on methods it does not use. Clients should not be forced to implement interfaces they don't use.
Dependency Inversion Principle: Depend on abstractions, not concretions. Use dependency injection repositories.
Don't Repeat Yourself: Eliminate code duplication. Consolidation of logic ensures a single source of truth.
Keep It Simple, Stupid: Avoid over-engineering. Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication in software engineering.
You Ain't Gonna Need It: Implement features only when needed, not when you foresee needing them. Focus on current requirements.
Leave the code cleaner than you found it. Refactor continuously as you work on features to prevent technical debt accumulation.
Names should be descriptive and unambiguous. Avoid short, cryptic labels; prefer readable, intent-revealing names.
